name: jq
description: jq — the JSON processor. Extract, query, filter, transform, and pretty-print JSON data. Read a JSON object or array from standard input and apply a jq filter expression to get fields, access values, select elements, count items, list keys, or reshape the structure. Supports dot notation to get a field (.field, .name, .price), array indexing (.[0], .[-1]), iteration (.[] to loop over elements), pipes (|), and built-in functions (length, keys, values, select, map, sort_by, group_by, unique, flatten, add, any, all, type, tostring, tonumber). Use for extracting values from JSON, querying API responses, counting elements in an array, getting nested fields, filtering objects by condition, or pretty-printing compact JSON.

input.description: JSON data on standard input. The first argument is the jq filter expression (e.g., '.name', '.[0]', '.items[] | .name', 'length').

output.description: The result of applying the jq filter to the input. Output format depends on the filter — may be a single value, array, or object.
